The morning was cold. James pulled his jacket closer and stepped out into the snow. He had walked this path a thousand times, but today it felt different. Today everything felt different.

By the time he reached the bridge, his breath was coming in clouds and his fingers had gone numb. He paused. The river below moved slowly under a thin sheet of ice.

He thought about turning back. He thought about a great many things, in fact, standing there on the bridge with the cold seeping into his bones. But in the end he kept walking, because there was nowhere else to go.
